query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
## Summary

**Did the US provide written notice of withdrawal under USMCA Article 34.6 around July 1, 2026? — No.**

On July 1, 2026, the USMCA Free Trade Commission held a virtual trilateral meeting for the mandatory six-year joint review. USTR **Jamieson Greer** announced that **"the United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form."** This was a **non-renewal under Article 34.7**, which is legally distinct from a withdrawal:

- **No Article 34.6 withdrawal notice was filed.** Withdrawal (which requires six months' written notice) was not invoked.
- The agreement **remains fully in force** and continues on current terms until its natural expiration on **July 1, 2036**.
- Non-renewal triggers an **annual joint review process** every year through 2036. The parties can still agree to a 16-year extension at any future review, resetting the term.
- USTR announced a **third round of bilateral talks with Mexico** for the week of **July 20, 2026**; Canada talks are expected.

This is confirmed by USTR's official statement and legal analyses from worldtradelaw.net (IELP), CSIS, White & Case, Brownstein, and Thompson Hine. While some media framed it as "starting a decade-long countdown to exit," the operative legal reality is non-renewal + annual reviews — **not** a withdrawal. President Trump has repeatedly floated exit threats, but no formal withdrawal has occurred.

sources.title: Ambassador Greer Issues Statement on the USMCA Joint Review
sources.snippet: The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is [not renewed]. Third round of bilateral negotiations with Mexico scheduled week of July 20.

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Not To Extend USMCA for Another Term: What Does That Mean Exactly?
sources.snippet: Non-renewal under Article 34.7, not withdrawal; Article 34.6 not invoked. USMCA remains in force until 2036 with annual reviews.

sources.title: The United States Has Opted Not to Extend the USMCA
sources.snippet: US opted not to renew for another 16 years; not a withdrawal. USMCA continues until it expires July 1, 2036. Annual reviews triggered; bilateral talks with Mexico initiated.

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Opts for Annual Review Process
sources.snippet: US formally declined to renew USMCA on July 1, triggering an annual review process that extends negotiations for up to a decade.

sources.title: USMCA 2026 Joint Review: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews
sources.snippet: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews.

sources.title: USTR Issues Statement on Status of USMCA Review
sources.snippet: USMCA entered into force July 1, 2020; sixth-anniversary review clause; US declined to renew in current form.

sources.title: USMCA Enters Uncertain Review Period
sources.snippet: On July 1, 2026, USTR Greer announced the US, Mexico and Canada failed to reach agreement to renew USMCA in its current form.

sources.title: Trump administration declines to renew USMCA trade deal
